description: Adversarial inputs for §5.4 IR + IrLowering pass
notes: Schema: expected_outcome has three values:
'compile_success'         — verifies + lowers cleanly
'verify_fail:<substring>' — fails at Verifier.verifyModule; substring in HumanRenderer output
'lower_fail:<substring>'  — verifies cleanly but IrLowering.lowerModule throws
IllegalStateException whose message contains <substring>
Test runner pattern (NOT via Main.run):
1. Parse source to ModuleAst.
2. Verifier.verifyModule(moduleAst, SymbolTable()) → VerifyResult.
3. If result.isSuccessful: IrLowering.lowerModule(moduleAst, result.resolvedTypes).
Catch IllegalStateException for lower_fail.
4. Compare outcome against expected_outcome.
CRITICAL IMPL FINDING (OQ-5.4-1): Undeclared identifiers do NOT cause lower_fail.
The spec-prompt stated undeclared identifiers → lower_fail.
ACTUAL BEHAVIOR: Elaboration.elaborateExpression for IDENTIFIER stores VoidType
when scope.lookup(name)==null (OQ-3=C). IrLowering reads resolvedTypes[expr]=VoidType
→ IrType.fromWaterfallType(VoidType)=IrType.Void. No throw. IrLowering.kt KDoc confirms:
'undeclared names lower to IrExpression.Identifier(x, IrType.Void) rather than throwing'.
The IrLowering.lowerExpression IDENTIFIER arm throws ONLY if resolvedTypes[expr] is null
— which never happens because Elaboration always writes at least VoidType.
neg-41 through neg-44 document undeclared-identifier cases with compile_success outcomes.
CRITICAL IMPL FINDING (OQ-5.4-2): Cast to unknown type IS a lower_fail.
IrLowering.lowerExpression for CAST calls WaterfallType.fromSourceText(castTargetType)
then IrType.fromWaterfallType(result). Unknown types → ErrorType → fromWaterfallType
throws IllegalStateException('Cannot lower error type to IR: <text>').
ExpressionVerifier is a P10 no-op so the verifier never catches bad cast targets.
neg-22 through neg-30 are the lower_fail entries via cast-to-unknown-type.
CAST TO VOID NOTE (OQ-5.4-3): Cast to 'void' does NOT cause lower_fail.
fromSourceText('void')=VoidType; fromWaterfallType(VoidType)=IrType.Void. No throw.
A void-cast silently produces Cast(targetType=IrType.Void, operand).
LAMBDA SYNTAX (OQ-5.4-6): Lambda arrow is '==>' NOT '->' (WaterfallLexer.g4: LAMBDA: '==>').
Full syntax: 'f := (int x) ==> body' — walrus ':=' for untyped var + '==>' arrow.
'var' is NOT a Waterfall keyword; untyped declarations use ':=' not 'var name ='.
EMPTY ARRAY LITERAL (OQ-5.4-7): '[]' is NOT parseable with the current grammar.
arrayLiteral grammar requires positionalArgumentList which requires >=1 expression.
'[]' causes a PARSE_ERROR. The Q3=Void path (IrType.Void for empty array) is dead code
from the grammar side. pos-15 is replaced with a char[] test instead.
FOR-LOOP ITERATOR (OQ-5.4-4): The R2 fix declares iterator in the body scope.
StatementVerifier.verifyForBlock declares iterator with IntType before walking body.
Elaboration.elaborateStatement for ForBlockData also declares iterator with IntType.
So 'for(item in things) { int x = item }' compiles cleanly in §5.4.
BINARY_OP type: left.type P10 placeholder. BinaryOp on Int operands → type=Int.
FUNCTION_CALL(LOCAL) undeclared: elaboration stores VoidType; lowering → type=Void. No throw.

open_questions.title: Undeclared identifier at lower time: silent Void vs. throw — spec/impl divergence
open_questions.description: The spec-prompt §5.4 claimed that 'module M { func f() { var y = undeclared } }' would cause IrLowering to throw IllegalStateException. ACTUAL IMPL: Elaboration.elaborateExpression stores WaterfallType.VoidType (not null) for undeclared identifiers. IrLowering reads VoidType from the side-table → IrType.Void. No throw. IrLowering.kt KDoc (line 18-19) explicitly documents this: 'undeclared names lower to IrExpression.Identifier(x, IrType.Void) rather than throwing.' The IllegalStateException in IrLowering for IDENTIFIER fires only if resolvedTypes[expr] is null — which never happens because Elaboration always writes at least VoidType. Negative entries neg-41 through neg-44 are therefore marked compile_success. Recommend: document in §3.7/§3.8 spec that undeclared → Void (not throw) is the P10 behavior; explicitly note the P11 fix will move detection to the verifier.
open_questions.status: OPEN — spec-prompt mismatch from actual impl; impl is consistent with OQ-3=C decision

open_questions.title: Cast to unknown type is an undetected lower_fail — P11 work
open_questions.description: Casting to any type not in the WaterfallType known set (e.g., 'n castas string') passes verification (ExpressionVerifier is P10 no-op) but throws at lowering via IrType.fromWaterfallType(ErrorType). This surfaces a class of programs that should have been caught at verify time. The exception message 'Cannot lower error type to IR: X. The verifier should have rejected this before lowering.' itself documents the gap. P11 should add cast-target-type validation to ExpressionVerifier, converting these from lower_fail to verify_fail.
open_questions.status: OPEN — documented P10 gap; P11 should move to verify_fail

open_questions.title: Cast to void does not error — silent wrong-type cast
open_questions.description: fromSourceText('void')=VoidType; IrType.fromWaterfallType(VoidType)=IrType.Void. A cast 'x castas void' in a return position verifies and lowers cleanly, producing Cast(targetType=IrType.Void). This is semantically wrong (void is not a value type) but P10 doesn't validate cast semantics. OQ-5.4-3 entry in notes covers this.
open_questions.status: OPEN — P10 gap; cast semantics check deferred to P11

open_questions.title: String literal lowers to IrType.Char — audit gap §3.6
open_questions.description: STRING_LITERAL lowers to IrExpression.StringLiteral with type=IrType.Char. This matches today's untyped-string-infers-to-char behavior but is an audit gap. §3.6 notes it explicitly. pos-24 exercises this path. Escalation §6.2-5 applies.
open_questions.status: OPEN — documented audit gap §3.6; no P10 fix

open_questions.title: For-loop iterator type is always Int in P10 (implicit-int convention)
open_questions.description: Elaboration and StatementVerifier both declare the for-loop iterator with WaterfallType.IntType. This is the P10 implicit-int convention. P11 will infer the proper element type from the collection expression. pos-09 and pos-33 exercise this path.
open_questions.status: OPEN — documented P10 convention; P11 closes

positive_entries.name: pos-01-top-level-int-var
positive_entries.rationale: Single top-level typed int var. lowerModule: IrTopLevelVariable(name=x, type=IrType.Int, isReadonly=false, initializer=IntLiteral('42')). Exercises Pass-1 top-level-vars path in lowerModule and the INT_LITERAL arm of lowerExpression.

positive_entries.name: pos-02-top-level-dec-var
positive_entries.rationale: Single top-level dec var. lowerExpression DEC_LITERAL → IrExpression.DecLiteral('1.5'). IrType.fromWaterfallType(DecType) = IrType.Dec. Exercises dec scalar lowering.

positive_entries.name: pos-03-top-level-bool-var
positive_entries.rationale: Single top-level bool var. BOOL_LITERAL 'true' → IrExpression.BoolLiteral(value=true). IrType.Bool is implicit on BoolLiteral.

positive_entries.name: pos-04-function-returning-int
positive_entries.rationale: Zero-param function returning int. IrFunction(name=answer, parameters=[], returnType=IrType.Int, body=[ReturnStatement(IntLiteral('42'))]). Exercises the basic function-lowering path.

positive_entries.name: pos-05-function-returning-array
positive_entries.rationale: Function returning int[]. returnType = IrType.Array(IrType.Int). Body: TypedVarDecl(xs, Array(Int), ArrayLiteral([IntLiteral(1),IntLiteral(2),IntLiteral(3)], Array(Int))). Return: Identifier(xs, Array(Int)) via elaboration. Exercises array type in return position + ArrayLiteral + Identifier resolution.

positive_entries.name: pos-06-function-mixed-params
positive_entries.rationale: Three parameters of distinct types. IrParameter list: [IrParameter(a, Int), IrParameter(b, Dec), IrParameter(c, Bool)]. Legacy Pair ordering firstVal=type/secondVal=name is swapped to name-first per §1.3. Return 'b' → Identifier(b, Dec). Adversarially targets PITFALL #8 parameter ordering inversion.

positive_entries.name: pos-07-if-elif-else-control-flow
positive_entries.rationale: Full if/elif/else lowering. IrStatement.IfBlock with ifBranch, one elifBranch, and elseBody. Each branch condition is Identifier(n, Int) via elaboration. Exercises all three IfBlock arms in lowerStatement.

positive_entries.name: pos-08-while-loop-lowering
positive_entries.rationale: While loop with decrement in body. WhileBlock(condition=Identifier(n, Int), body=[IncrementStatement(n, --)]. Tests while condition elaboration and body lowering.

positive_entries.name: pos-09-for-loop-iterator-use-in-body
positive_entries.rationale: For-loop body using the iterator variable. R2 fix: verifyForBlock declares 'item' with IntType into the body scope; Elaboration also declares 'item' as IntType. 'int x = item' verifies cleanly and lowers to TypedVarDecl(x, Int, Identifier(item, Int)). Different from §5.3 where iterator was NOT declared. Per OQ-5.4-5 iterator type is always Int in P10.

positive_entries.name: pos-10-recursive-fibonacci
positive_entries.rationale: Recursive function. ModuleVerifier self-decls 'fib' before body elaboration, so LOCAL call to 'fib' resolves. Elaboration stores fib's return type (IntType). Return: BinaryOp(+, FunctionCall(Local, fib, [BinaryOp(-, n, IntLiteral(1))]), FunctionCall(Local, fib, [BinaryOp(-, n, IntLiteral(2))]), type=Int). BinaryOp type = left.type placeholder.

positive_entries.name: pos-11-cross-function-call
positive_entries.rationale: Function calling a sibling declared before it. In 'compute', LOCAL call to 'helper': elaboration calls scope.lookup('helper').type = IntType (function's return type). FunctionCall(Local, helper, [Identifier(x, Int)], type=Int). Exercises cross-function LOCAL call type resolution.

positive_entries.name: pos-12-local-var-multiple-assignments
positive_entries.rationale: Local var used in multiple assignment statements. Produces: TypedVarDecl(total, Int, IntLiteral(0)), VarAssignment(total, +=, IntLiteral(5)), VarAssignment(total, -=, IntLiteral(2)), VarAssignment(total, *=, IntLiteral(3)). Tests multi-statement body and compound-op preservation in lowerStatement.

positive_entries.name: pos-13-lambda-assignment-and-use
positive_entries.rationale: Lambda assigned via walrus operator (syntax: 'f := (int x) ==> body'). Lambda arrow is '==>' not '->' (WaterfallLexer LAMBDA token). LAMBDA ExpressionData. Elaboration: child scope with param 'x' (Int); elaborates body FunctionCall add(x, 1). lowerExpression for LAMBDA: params=[IrParameter(x, Int)], body=FunctionCall(Local, add, [Identifier(x, Int), IntLiteral(1)], type=Void), overall type=IrType.Void placeholder. Exercises LAMBDA lowering and lambda-scope elaboration.

positive_entries.name: pos-14-array-literals-all-scalars
positive_entries.rationale: Array literals of int, dec, and bool. Each ArrayLiteral: type from first element. ArrayIndex: type = resolvedTypes[arrayIndexExpr] = element type of the array (set by Elaboration from scope.lookup). Exercises INT/DEC/BOOL ArrayLiteral + ArrayIndex lowering for all three scalar element types.

positive_entries.name: pos-15-array-literal-char-type
positive_entries.rationale: String literal (char array) + array index returning char. NOTE: The Q3=Void path (empty array literal []) is exercised by the implementation but is UNREACHABLE from the current grammar (positionalArgumentList requires at least one expression; [] parses as PARSE_ERROR). This test is a replacement that exercises the ArrayIndex on a char[] instead. OQ-5.4-3 applies: string literal lowers to StringLiteral with type=IrType.Char (audit gap §3.6).

positive_entries.name: pos-16-binary-ops-arithmetic
positive_entries.rationale: Multiple binary arithmetic ops. Each BINARY_OP: type = lowerExpression(left).type (P10 placeholder). a+b → BinaryOp(+, Identifier(a,Int), Identifier(b,Int), type=Int). Tests three BINARY_OP lowerings and chained identifier references.

positive_entries.name: pos-17-binary-op-boolean-equals
positive_entries.rationale: Boolean 'equals' binary op in if condition. BINARY_OP(equals, Identifier(a,Int), Identifier(b,Int), type=Int) as the condition expression. Tests that lowerExpression is called recursively for the condition in IfBlock.

positive_entries.name: pos-18-cast-expression
positive_entries.rationale: Cast int to dec. CAST: castTargetType='dec'. fromSourceText('dec')=DecType; fromWaterfallType(DecType)=IrType.Dec. Cast(targetType=Dec, operand=Identifier(n, Int)). Exercises the CAST lowering path with a valid target type.

positive_entries.name: pos-19-const-and-imm-vars
positive_entries.rationale: const and imm modifiers. R1 fix: isReadonly = s.isImmutable(). TypedVarDecl(limit, Int, isReadonly=true), TypedVarDecl(threshold, Dec, isReadonly=true), TypedVarDecl(count, Int, isReadonly=false). Verifies both modifier keywords set isReadonly=true in IR.

positive_entries.name: pos-20-empty-function-body
positive_entries.rationale: Two functions with empty bodies. IrFunction(body=[]). body.map{lowerStatement} on an empty list returns an empty list cleanly. Tests the zero-statement function path through lowerModule.

positive_entries.name: pos-21-deeply-nested-expression
positive_entries.rationale: Deeply nested arithmetic expression (a + (b * c)) - d. Produces nested BinaryOps. lowerExpression recurses into both sides at each level. Tests multi-level recursive lowerExpression for BINARY_OP without stack overflow or traversal error.

positive_entries.name: pos-22-top-level-const-and-imm
positive_entries.rationale: const and imm top-level vars. lowerModule Pass-1: IrTopLevelVariable(MAX, Int, isReadonly=true), IrTopLevelVariable(PI, Dec, isReadonly=true). R1 fix applies at module-level top-level-var lowering. Tests isImmutable() for top-level vars.

positive_entries.name: pos-23-walrus-untyped-vars
positive_entries.rationale: Walrus-operator declarations of int, dec, bool literals. inferType: INT_LITERAL→'int', DEC_LITERAL→'dec', BOOL_LITERAL→'int' (default fallback). IrStatement.UntypedVarDecl(x, Int), UntypedVarDecl(y, Dec), UntypedVarDecl(z, Int). Note: BOOL_LITERAL → 'int' due to inferType fallback (not a bug in fixtures — inferType is limited in P10). Exercises UntypedVarDecl lowering.

positive_entries.name: pos-24-string-literal-lowers-to-char
positive_entries.rationale: String literal with backtick syntax. STRING_LITERAL → StringLiteral(literalText='`hello world`', type=IrType.Char). Elaboration: STRING_LITERAL → WaterfallType.CharType. ArrayIndex on msg: type=CharType from elaboration. Exercises OQ-5.4-4 string→char audit gap (§3.6).

positive_entries.name: pos-25-null-literal
positive_entries.rationale: Null literal assigned via walrus. inferType for NULL_LITERAL returns 'int' (default). Elaboration: NULL_LITERAL → WaterfallType.VoidType. lowerExpression: NullLiteral(type=IrType.Void). Exercises the NULL_LITERAL path through lowerExpression.

positive_entries.name: pos-26-function-call-statement
positive_entries.rationale: Function-call in statement position. FunctionCallStatementData wraps a LOCAL call to 'doSomething'. IrStatement.FunctionCallStatement(FunctionCall(Local, doSomething, [], [], type=Void)). 'doSomething' undeclared but per OQ-3=C/OQ-5.4-1 elaboration stores VoidType — lowers silently. Exercises FunctionCallStatement path.

positive_entries.name: pos-27-module-call-statement
positive_entries.rationale: MODULE-kind function call. Kind.MODULE: maps to FunctionCall.Kind.Module. moduleName='Other', functionName='doSomething', args=[IntLiteral(1), IntLiteral(2)]. Return type=Void (R3 edge case: non-LOCAL kinds use Void placeholder). Exercises the MODULE call path.

positive_entries.name: pos-28-while-with-local-vars
positive_entries.rationale: While loop with local vars used inside. 'i' and 'total' in function scope; while condition BinaryOp(<, Identifier(i), Identifier(n)). Body: VarAssignment(total, +=, Identifier(i)) and IncrementStatement(i, ++). Elaboration's two-pass ensures both vars visible during body elaboration. Tests compound while + local-var interaction.

positive_entries.name: pos-29-increment-decrement
positive_entries.rationale: Both increment and decrement operators. IncrementStatement(name=x, op=++) and IncrementStatement(x, op=--). op preserved verbatim. No expression sub-tree in IncrementStatement. Exercises both arms of the increment lowering.

positive_entries.name: pos-30-bare-return
positive_entries.rationale: Bare return statement (value=null). ReturnStatementData.value==null. lowerStatement: IrStatement.ReturnStatement(value=null). Tests the null-value path in ReturnStatement lowering.

positive_entries.name: pos-31-empty-module
positive_entries.source:
module Pos31 {
}
positive_entries.rationale: Completely empty module. lowerModule: topLevelVariables=[], functions=[]. modulePos uses the fallback SourcePosition('(module)', 1, 0). Tests the zero-work path through lowerModule and the Elvis-chain fallback for modulePos.

positive_entries.name: pos-32-array-index-with-identifier-index
positive_entries.rationale: Array index where the index expression is an IDENTIFIER. ArrayIndex: target='arr', index=Identifier(i, Int). Elaboration: arrayType=scope.lookup('arr').type=ArrayType(IntType) → element=IntType for arrayIndex expr. lowerExpression ARRAY_INDEX: type=IrType.Int, index=Identifier(i, Int). Tests non-literal index in ArrayIndex.

positive_entries.name: pos-33-for-loop-scope-isolation
positive_entries.rationale: Same iterator name and body var in two sequential for loops. Each body scope is independent. Elaboration creates a fresh child scope per loop; 'item' re-declared in each. Tests sequential for-loop scope independence at the lowering layer.

negative_entries.name: neg-01-duplicate-toplevel-var
negative_entries.rationale: Carry-forward from §5.3. Duplicate top-level var name. Second declare finds 'x' in module scope → DuplicateDeclaration(topLevel=false) → 'Duplicate declaration: x'. Fails at verify time.

negative_entries.name: neg-02-duplicate-inner-var
negative_entries.rationale: Carry-forward §5.3. Duplicate var in function body. Second declare → DuplicateDeclaration → verify_fail.

negative_entries.name: neg-03-shadow-function-param
negative_entries.rationale: Carry-forward §5.3. Inner var shadows function parameter. Declare in child scope finds 'n' via parent chain → DuplicateDeclaration → verify_fail.

negative_entries.name: neg-04-duplicate-function-param-names
negative_entries.rationale: Carry-forward §5.3. Duplicate parameter names. Second param declare finds 'a' in functionScope → DuplicateDeclaration → verify_fail.

negative_entries.name: neg-05-assign-to-const
negative_entries.rationale: Carry-forward §5.3. AssignToReadonly: const var. verifyVarAssignment isReadonly=true → AssignToReadonly → verify_fail.

negative_entries.name: neg-06-assign-to-function-name-pitfall7
negative_entries.rationale: Carry-forward §5.3. PITFALL #7: function name is implicitly readonly. verifyVarAssignment isReadonly=true → verify_fail.

negative_entries.name: neg-07-void-variable-type
negative_entries.rationale: Carry-forward §5.3 (OQ-5.3-1 fixed). VoidNotAValueType: verifyTypedVarDecl emits VoidNotAValueType when type==VoidType → 'not a value type'. Fixed in §5.3 impl. Fails at verify; never reaches lowering.

negative_entries.name: neg-08-unknown-return-type
negative_entries.rationale: Carry-forward §5.3. Unknown return type 'string' → UnknownType → verify_fail.

negative_entries.name: neg-09-duplicate-function-names
negative_entries.rationale: Carry-forward §5.3. Duplicate function names. Second self-decl finds 'helper' in module scope → DuplicateDeclaration(topLevel=true) → verify_fail.

negative_entries.name: neg-10-increment-imm-var
negative_entries.rationale: Carry-forward §5.3. IncrementOfReadonly via imm modifier → verify_fail.

negative_entries.name: neg-11-assign-toplevel-const-from-function
negative_entries.rationale: Carry-forward §5.3. Const top-level var assigned from function body. Lookup walks parent chain, finds isReadonly=true → AssignToReadonly → verify_fail.

negative_entries.name: neg-12-inner-var-shadows-toplevel
negative_entries.rationale: Carry-forward §5.3. Inner var shadows top-level var. Declare in child scope finds 'x' via parent chain → DuplicateDeclaration → verify_fail.

negative_entries.name: neg-13-error-inside-if-branch
negative_entries.rationale: Carry-forward §5.3. Assign to const inside if-then branch. JoinAnalysis walks branch; AssignToReadonly → verify_fail.

negative_entries.name: neg-14-error-inside-while-body
negative_entries.rationale: Carry-forward §5.3. Duplicate var inside while body → DuplicateDeclaration → verify_fail.

negative_entries.name: neg-15-error-inside-for-body
negative_entries.rationale: Carry-forward §5.3. Assign to const inside for body. verifyForBlock walks body; AssignToReadonly → verify_fail.

negative_entries.name: neg-16-unknown-type-in-function-body
negative_entries.rationale: Carry-forward §5.3. Unknown type 'string' in function body → UnknownType → verify_fail.

negative_entries.name: neg-17-duplicate-walrus-vars
negative_entries.rationale: Carry-forward §5.3. Duplicate walrus declarations → DuplicateDeclaration → verify_fail.

negative_entries.name: neg-18-deeply-nested-error
negative_entries.rationale: Carry-forward §5.3. Assign to const at 3-level nesting depth. Error propagates through all three nesting levels → verify_fail.

negative_entries.name: neg-19-void-top-level-var
negative_entries.rationale: Carry-forward §5.3 (OQ-5.3-1 fix). Void used as top-level variable type → VoidNotAValueType → verify_fail.

negative_entries.name: neg-20-param-shadows-function-name
negative_entries.rationale: Carry-forward §5.3. Parameter has same name as function. Self-decl puts 'f' in module scope; per-arg declare in child finds 'f' via parent chain → DuplicateDeclaration → verify_fail.

negative_entries.name: neg-21-while-body-shadows-outer-var
negative_entries.rationale: Carry-forward §5.3. While-body var shadows enclosing function var. JoinAnalysis creates child scope; declare('n') finds 'n' via parent → DuplicateDeclaration → verify_fail.

negative_entries.name: neg-22-cast-to-unknown-type-in-return
negative_entries.expected_outcome: lower_fail:Cannot lower error type to IR: string
negative_entries.rationale: OQ-5.4-2. Cast to unknown type 'string' in return expression. ExpressionVerifier is P10 no-op so verify succeeds. Elaboration for CAST: fromSourceText('string')=ErrorType('string') stored in table. lowerExpression for CAST: WaterfallType.fromSourceText('string')=ErrorType('string') → IrType.fromWaterfallType(ErrorType('string')) throws IllegalStateException('Cannot lower error type to IR: string. The verifier should have rejected this before lowering.').

negative_entries.name: neg-23-cast-to-nullable-type
negative_entries.expected_outcome: lower_fail:Cannot lower error type to IR: ?int
negative_entries.rationale: OQ-5.4-2. Cast to nullable type '?int'. fromSourceText('?int'): text starts with '?' → ErrorType('?int') (audit surprise #5). inferType for CAST returns 'int' default → UntypedVarDecl.inferredType='int' → verifier happy. lowerExpression for CAST throws on ErrorType('?int').

negative_entries.name: neg-24-cast-to-multidim-array
negative_entries.expected_outcome: lower_fail:Cannot lower error type to IR: int[][]
negative_entries.rationale: OQ-5.4-2. Cast to 'int[][]'. fromSourceText('int[][]'): isArray=true, base='int[]', base not a known primitive key → ErrorType('int[][]'). lowerExpression CAST throws on ErrorType. Verify passes (P10 no-op). Tests multi-dimensional array type path.

negative_entries.name: neg-25-cast-to-void-array
negative_entries.expected_outcome: lower_fail:Cannot lower error type to IR: void[]
negative_entries.rationale: OQ-5.4-2. Cast to 'void[]'. fromSourceText('void[]'): isArray=true, base='void', baseType=VoidType; WaterfallType.kt line 79 guard: 'if (isArray && baseType == VoidType) return ErrorType(text)' → ErrorType('void[]'). lowerExpression CAST throws. Verify passes. Tests the void-array guard in WaterfallType.fromSourceText.

negative_entries.name: neg-26-cast-unknown-inside-if-branch
negative_entries.expected_outcome: lower_fail:Cannot lower error type to IR: florp
negative_entries.rationale: OQ-5.4-2. Cast to 'florp' inside if-branch body. Elaboration walks branch body; CAST stores ErrorType('florp'). lowerStatement for IfBlock recursively lowers the branch body, hits CAST lowerExpression → throws. Tests that lower_fail propagates out of nested if-branch.

negative_entries.name: neg-27-cast-unknown-inside-while-body
negative_entries.expected_outcome: lower_fail:Cannot lower error type to IR: sprocket
negative_entries.rationale: OQ-5.4-2. Cast to 'sprocket' inside while body. inferType for CAST → 'int' (default) → UntypedVarDecl.inferredType='int' → verifier happy. Elaboration stores ErrorType('sprocket') for CAST. lowerStatement WhileBlock iterates body, hits CAST lowerExpression → throws. Tests lower_fail from while-body.

negative_entries.name: neg-28-cast-unknown-inside-for-body
negative_entries.expected_outcome: lower_fail:Cannot lower error type to IR: sprocket
negative_entries.rationale: OQ-5.4-2. Cast to 'sprocket' inside for body. Elaboration for ForBlock declares iterator 'item', elaborates the UntypedVarDecl CAST value → ErrorType('sprocket'). lowerStatement ForBlock iterates body → CAST lowerExpression → throws. Tests lower_fail from for-body.

negative_entries.name: neg-29-cast-unknown-inside-lambda-body
negative_entries.expected_outcome: lower_fail:Cannot lower error type to IR: widget
negative_entries.rationale: OQ-5.4-2. Cast to 'widget' inside lambda body function call argument. Elaboration for LAMBDA: child scope, param 'x'; elaborateFunctionCall(convert(x castas widget)) → elaborateExpression(x castas widget) → CAST → ErrorType('widget') stored. lowerExpression LAMBDA → lowerFunctionCall → lowerExpression(x castas widget) → CAST → throws. Tests lower_fail propagation from inside lambda body.

negative_entries.name: neg-30-cast-unknown-in-if-condition
negative_entries.expected_outcome: lower_fail:Cannot lower error type to IR: sprocket
negative_entries.rationale: OQ-5.4-2. Cast to 'sprocket' in the if-block condition expression. Elaboration for IfBlock elaborates the condition (CAST) → ErrorType('sprocket'). lowerStatement IfBlock calls lowerExpression(ifBranch.condition) → CAST → throws. Tests lower_fail from the condition expression position (not just branch body).

negative_entries.name: neg-31-cast-unknown-in-binary-op
negative_entries.expected_outcome: lower_fail:Cannot lower error type to IR: flim
negative_entries.rationale: OQ-5.4-2. Cast to 'flim' nested as left operand of a binary op. lowerExpression BINARY_OP recurses into left = CAST(n, 'flim') → throws. Tests lower_fail propagation from inside a binary op sub-expression.

negative_entries.name: neg-32-cast-to-unknown-array-type
negative_entries.expected_outcome: lower_fail:Cannot lower error type to IR: widget[]
negative_entries.rationale: OQ-5.4-2. Cast to unknown array type 'widget[]'. fromSourceText('widget[]'): isArray=true, base='widget' → ErrorType('widget[]'). lowerExpression CAST throws. Tests cast-to-array-of-unknown-type path.

negative_entries.name: neg-33-assign-imm-inside-for-body
negative_entries.rationale: Assign to imm var inside for-loop body. verifyForBlock creates body scope, declares iterator, walks body: verifyTypedVarDecl(imm int x) → isReadonly=true; verifyVarAssignment(x=2) → AssignToReadonly → verify_fail. Tests that imm modifier is respected inside for-loop bodies.

negative_entries.name: neg-34-void-var-in-elif-branch
negative_entries.rationale: Void variable type inside elif branch. JoinAnalysis walks elifBranches; verifyTypedVarDecl('void bad') → VoidNotAValueType → verify_fail. Tests VoidNotAValueType check fires correctly inside elif bodies.

negative_entries.name: neg-35-unknown-toplevel-type
negative_entries.rationale: Unknown type 'string' at top-level. verifyTypedVarDecl (Pass 1): fromSourceText('string')=ErrorType → UnknownType → verify_fail. Lowering not attempted.

negative_entries.name: neg-36-cast-unknown-in-while-condition
negative_entries.expected_outcome: lower_fail:Cannot lower error type to IR: bogus
negative_entries.rationale: OQ-5.4-2. Cast to 'bogus' in while condition. Elaboration elaborates the condition CAST → ErrorType('bogus'). lowerStatement WhileBlock calls lowerExpression(condition) → CAST → throws. Tests lower_fail propagation from while-condition position.

negative_entries.name: neg-37-cast-to-unknown-chain
negative_entries.expected_outcome: lower_fail:Cannot lower error type to IR: sprocket
negative_entries.rationale: OQ-5.4-2. First cast 'n castas dec' is valid (lowers to Cast(Dec, Identifier(n, Int))). Second cast 'd castas sprocket' is unknown. lowerExpression encounters the second CAST, throws. Tests that a chain of casts fails on the first invalid one, not the whole expression.

negative_entries.name: neg-38-module-call-with-cast-arg
negative_entries.expected_outcome: lower_fail:Cannot lower error type to IR: splat
negative_entries.rationale: OQ-5.4-2. Cast to 'splat' as argument to a MODULE-kind function call. lowerFunctionCall iterates positionalArguments and calls lowerExpression on each. The argument 'n castas splat' is a CAST → ErrorType('splat') → throws. Tests lower_fail inside module-call arguments.

negative_entries.name: neg-39-cast-unknown-in-var-assignment
negative_entries.expected_outcome: lower_fail:Cannot lower error type to IR: sprocket
negative_entries.rationale: OQ-5.4-2. Cast to 'sprocket' as the value of a variable assignment. lowerStatement for VarAssignment calls lowerExpression(stmt.value) = CAST(n, 'sprocket') → ErrorType → throws. Tests lower_fail from assignment right-hand side expression.

negative_entries.name: neg-40-deeply-nested-cast-in-nested-control-flow
negative_entries.expected_outcome: lower_fail:Cannot lower error type to IR: bogotype
negative_entries.rationale: OQ-5.4-2. Cast to 'bogotype' at 3-level nesting depth (if→while→for). Tests that lower_fail propagates correctly through three levels of nested control-flow lowering, mirroring the neg-18 pattern but for lower_fail instead of verify_fail.

negative_entries.name: neg-41-undeclared-identifier-silently-void
negative_entries.rationale: OQ-5.4-1 DOCUMENTATION: spec-prompt claimed lower_fail; actual behavior is compile_success. Elaboration stores VoidType for undeclared 'undeclared' identifier. IrLowering reads VoidType → Identifier('undeclared', IrType.Void). No throw. TypedVarDecl(y, Int, Identifier(undeclared, Void)). Verifier also does NOT reject this (OQ-3=C). Result: semantically wrong IR produced silently. This is the documented P10 gap.

negative_entries.name: neg-42-undeclared-function-call-silently-void
negative_entries.rationale: OQ-5.4-1 DOCUMENTATION: LOCAL call to undeclared function 'nope'. Elaboration for FUNCTION_CALL(LOCAL): scope.lookup('nope')=null → VoidType stored. lowerExpression FUNCTION_CALL: returnType=VoidType → IrType.Void; no throw. FunctionCall(Local, nope, [IntLiteral(5)], type=Void). compile_success.

negative_entries.name: neg-43-undeclared-array-index-silently-void
negative_entries.rationale: OQ-5.4-1 DOCUMENTATION: Array index on undeclared target 'unknown'. Elaboration for ARRAY_INDEX: scope.lookup('unknown')=null → arrayType=null → VoidType stored. lowerExpression ARRAY_INDEX: elementType=IrType.Void. Produces ArrayIndex(target='unknown', index=IntLiteral(0), type=IrType.Void). No throw. compile_success.

negative_entries.name: neg-44-lambda-body-refs-undeclared-silently-void
negative_entries.rationale: OQ-5.4-1 DOCUMENTATION: Lambda body calls undeclared 'nope'. Elaboration for LAMBDA: child scope with param 'x'; elaborateFunctionCall(nope(x), lambdaScope) → scope.lookup('nope')=null → VoidType. lowerExpression LAMBDA: body=FunctionCall(Local, nope, [Identifier(x,Int)], type=Void). No throw. compile_success. All four neg-41 through neg-44 entries document the OQ-5.4-1 gap.
